label: Gusto
description: Connect Gusto to read and manage payroll, benefits, and HR data for your organization.

form.title: Connect Gusto
form.subtitle: Authorize Anton to access your Gusto payroll, benefits, and HR data.

methods.label: OAuth 2.0 (Bring Your Own App)
methods.description: Connect via Gusto's OAuth 2.0 flow using a developer app you create at dev.gusto.com. Anton opens Gusto's consent screen in your browser — no passwords stored, revocable at any time.
methods.how_to:
## Connect Gusto via OAuth 2.0

Gusto requires you to create your own developer application to obtain OAuth credentials. This takes about 5 minutes.

### 1. Create a Gusto Developer App

1. Go to [dev.gusto.com](https://dev.gusto.com) and sign in or create a developer account.
2. Click **Applications → Create Application**.
3. Give it a name like `Anton` and fill in a description.
4. Under **Redirect URIs**, add `http://localhost` (Anton uses a local redirect to capture the code).
5. Click **Create**.

### 2. Copy your credentials

1. Open your newly created app and navigate to the **OAuth** or **Credentials** tab.
2. Copy the **Client ID** and **Client Secret** — the secret is shown once, so copy it now.
3. Paste both into the fields below.

### 3. Authorize

1. Click **Submit**. Anton opens Gusto's consent screen in your browser.
2. Log in with the Gusto account you want to connect and click **Authorize**.
3. Anton exchanges the authorization code for a refresh token and stores it locally.

### Notes

- Use the **Demo** environment at `https://api.gusto-demo.com` during testing; switch to `https://api.gusto.com` for production.
- Scopes map to Gusto's resource permissions — if you need narrower access, remove scopes you don't need before submitting.
- To revoke access, go to Gusto → **Account Settings → Authorized Applications** and remove the entry.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: Client ID
fields.description: Found in your app's Credentials tab at dev.gusto.com.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: Client Secret
fields.description: Paired with the Client ID. Copy it immediately after creating the app — Gusto may only show it once.

fields.name: environment
fields.label: Environment
fields.description: Use Demo for testing with sandbox data; Production for live payroll data.
